template_name: Standard Prescription Format - Antenatally Detected Hydronephrosis

condition.name: Antenatally Detected Hydronephrosis
overview: Hydronephrosis is the most common urinary tract anomaly detected on antenatal ultrasound.
It refers to dilation of the renal pelvis with or without calyceal involvement.
Most cases are transient and resolve spontaneously, but some indicate obstruction or vesicoureteral reflux (VUR).

postnatal_followup_timing: First renal ultrasound at 3–7 days of life
Repeat ultrasound at 4–6 weeks if abnormal

grading_sfu_system.finding: Pelvis and all calyces dilated, parenchyma preserved
Severe dilation with thinning of renal parenchyma

investigations.name: VCUG (Voiding Cystourethrogram)
investigations.indication: Bilateral dilation, ureteral dilation, abnormal bladder, or UTI

parent_counseling: Regular follow-up and scans are essential.
Watch for signs of UTI such as fever, irritability, or poor feeding.
Serial imaging helps guide treatment decisions.

summary_table.next_step: No further action; repeat USG if suspicion remains

flow_logic: If Grade 1–2 → repeat ultrasound at 6 weeks
If Grade 3–4 or bilateral → VCUG and nuclear scan
Refer to pediatric urology/nephrology if abnormal findings
quick_checklist_for_opd: Any UTI history or abnormal bladder signs?
Referral done for high-grade or symptomatic cases?
counselling: Most cases resolve without surgery — ज़्यादातर मामले बिना ऑपरेशन के ठीक हो जाते हैं
Regular follow-up and scans are essential — नियमित जांच और अल्ट्रासाउंड ज़रूरी है
Watch for signs of UTI such as fever, irritability, or poor feeding — पेशाब के संक्रमण के संकेत देखें जैसे बुखार, चिड़चिड़ापन, या कम दूध पीना
Serial imaging helps guide treatment decisions — बार-बार की जाने वाली तस्वीरें इलाज का फैसला करने में मदद करती हैं
Early detection prevents kidney damage — जल्दी पता लगने से गुर्दे की क्षति रुकती है
warning_signs: Fever or irritability suggesting UTI — बुखार या चिड़चिड़ापन जो पेशाब के संक्रमण का संकेत हो
Poor feeding or vomiting — कम दूध पीना या उल्टी
Palpable abdominal mass — पेट में गांठ महसूस होना
Poor urine stream or dribbling — पेशाब की कमज़ोर धार या टपकना
Failure to thrive — वज़न और विकास न बढ़ना
Decreased urine output — पेशाब कम आना
expected_course: Mild hydronephrosis (Grade 1-2): resolves spontaneously in 50-70% of cases by 12-18 months. Moderate hydronephrosis (Grade 3): requires close monitoring; may need surgery if worsening. Severe hydronephrosis (Grade 4): often requires surgical intervention (pyeloplasty). PUJ obstruction is the most common pathological cause. Postnatal ultrasound should be done after day 3.
key_clinical_points: First postnatal ultrasound at 3-7 days of life (not immediately - early scan may be falsely reassuring)
SFU grading system: Grade 1-4 based on pelvic and calyceal dilation
Grade 1-2: serial ultrasound monitoring; no routine antibiotic prophylaxis
Grade 3-4: antibiotic prophylaxis, VCUG, nuclear scan (DTPA/MAG3)
Bilateral hydronephrosis or abnormal bladder: rule out posterior urethral valves
Check serum creatinine at day 3-5 (reflects neonatal renal function, not maternal)
UTI prevention with antibiotic prophylaxis in high-grade cases
Refer to pediatric urologist/nephrologist for Grade 3-4 or bilateral cases

severity_assessment.mild: Grade 1-2 unilateral hydronephrosis, normal renal parenchyma - serial ultrasound at 6 weeks, 3 months, 6 months
severity_assessment.moderate: Grade 3 or bilateral Grade 2, no parenchymal thinning - antibiotic prophylaxis, VCUG, consider nuclear scan, pediatric nephrology referral
severity_assessment.severe: Grade 4, parenchymal thinning, bilateral involvement, abnormal bladder, elevated creatinine - urgent urology referral, full imaging workup, possible surgical intervention

monitoring_parameters.frequency: Day 3-7, then 4-6 weeks, then 3 months, 6 months, 12 months (adjust based on grade)
Day 3-5 for bilateral or high-grade; repeat if abnormal
If fever or UTI symptoms; consider periodic screening in high-grade cases
